org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge

Class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.AbstractPropertyTooler
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.view.AbstractViewComponent
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.AbstractFigure
          • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.AbstractModelObjectFigure<E>
            • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.EdgeFigure<E>
  • Type Parameters:
    E - is the type of the model edge supported by this figure.
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<ViewComponent>, Selectable, PropertyOwner, Figure, ModelObjectFigure<E>, LinearFeature, ModelObjectView<E>, ViewComponent, ViewComponentConstants, PropertyNames, View
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    PolylineEdgeFigure, SegmentEdgeFigure

    public abstract class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
extends AbstractModelObjectFigure<E>
implements LinearFeature
    Abstract Figure classe for representing edges between nodes' ports.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:01
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND, Mahdi HANNOUN
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-fig

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      protected static class  EdgeFigure.PathDetails
      A container for a path and the tangents of the path ends.
      protected class  EdgeFigure.SPainter

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void cleanUp()
      Clean this Fig and all its internal temprary fields.
      protected Rectangle2f computeEdgeBounds()
      Invoked to refine the computation of the bounds of the edge's segments.
      protected abstract EdgeFigure.PathDetails computePath(List<? extends Point2D> controlPoints)
      Create and replies the general path that is representing this edge figure.
      protected void computeSegmentLengths(float[] lengths)
      Compute the lengths of all the segments and put these lengths in the specified array.
      boolean contains(float x, float y)
      Reply true if given coords are in the bounds of this Fig.
      boolean contains(Rectangle2f r)
      Reply true if the given rectangle is inside this component.
      protected EdgeShadowPainter createShadowPainter()
      Invoked by AbstractFigure.getShadowPainter() when an instance of shadow painter must be create.
      protected abstract float distanceToSegment(float x1, float y1, float x2, float y2, float x, float y, Point2D pts)
      Replies if a point is closed to a segment.
      (package private) Point2D findConnectingPointFromTo(Anchor<?,?,?,?> from, Anchor<?,?,?,?> to, boolean isFromStart)
      Compute and replies the position of the end of the edge that is connected to the anchor "from" and the other side of the edge is connected to the anchor "to".
      abstract boolean flatteningAt(int index)
      Try to remove the control point at the specified index if its removal does produce a large change in the polyline.
      Point2f getCtrlPointAt(int index)
      return the point of the path point edge defined by the parameter.
      int getCtrlPointCount()
      Return the number of point in the path of the edge.
      List<? extends Point2D> getCtrlPoints()
      Get the edge control points.
      float getDistanceToSegmentAt(int index)
      Replies the distance of the first point of the segment at the specified index from the beginning of the edge.
      EdgeSymbol getEndSymbol()
      Reply the end-symbol connected to the target port.
      UnmodifiablePoint2f getFirstCtrlPoint()
      Get The source port point.
      UnmodifiablePoint2f getLastCtrlPoint()
      Get the destination port point.
      float getLength()
      Replies the size of the edge.
      abstract int getMaxAllowedCtrlPoints()
      Return the max number of control point for the edge.
      int getNearestCtrlPointTo(float x, float y)
      Replies the index of the nearest control point to the specified point.
      Point2D getNearestPointTo(float x, float y)
      Replies the point on the linear feature that is near to the given coordinates.
      int getNearestSegmentTo(float x, float y)
      Replies the index of the nearest segment to the specified point.
      Path2f getPath()
      Replies the general path that is representing this edge figure.
      PathIterator2f getPathIterator()
      Replies the path iterator of the edge.
      Map<String,Object> getProperties()
      Set<ResizeDirection> getResizeDirections()
      Reply the directions this Fig can be resized by the user
      float getSegmentLength(int index)
      Replies the size of the segment at the specified index.
      EdgeShadowPainter getShadowPainter()
      Replies the painter that may be used to paint this figure in shadow mode.
      EdgeSymbol getStartSymbol()
      Reply the end-symbol connected to the source port.
      Iterable<? extends SubFigure> getSubFigures()
      Replies the sub figures.
      Vector2D getTangentAtFirstPoint()
      Replies the tangent at the first point.
      Vector2D getTangentAtLastPoint()
      Replies the tangent at the last point.
      int hitCtrlPoint(float x, float y, float epsilon)
      Replies the hit control point at the specified position.
      int hitSegment(float x, float y, float epsilon)
      Replies the index of the nearest segment to the specified point.
      void insertCtrlPointAt(int index, float x, float y)
      Insert a point into the path defined by the given index.
      boolean intersects(Shape2f r)
      Reply true if the object intersects the given shape.
      boolean isClosed()
      Replies if the linear feature is closed.
      protected abstract boolean isClosedToSegment(float x1, float y1, float x2, float y2, float x, float y, float hitDistance)
      Replies if a point is closed to a segment.

      Method to paint this Fig.
      protected void paintEdgeSymbols(ViewGraphics2D g)
      Method to paint end-symbols of this Fig.

      Paint the segments represented by the specified control points.
      void refreshConnectedCtrlPoints()
      Refresh the positions of the connected control points.
      void removeCtrlPointAt(int index)
      Remove a point at the given index from the path.

      Change the position and the dimension of this Fig.

      Set the point of the path defined by the given index.

      Set the control points of the edge.

      Set the control points of the edge.

      Change the end-symbol connected to the target port.

      Set the source port point.

      Set the destination port point.

      Change the position of this Fig.

      Set the directions the figure can be resized by the user to all the available directions.

      Set the directions the figure can be resized by the user
      void setStartSymbol(EdgeSymbol symbol)
      Change the end-symbol connected to the source port.

      Move this Fig.

      Invoked to update the geometry of any associated object when the shape of this component has changed.

      Invoked when the associated has model changed.

      Calculate the bounds of the edge and its associated symbols.

      Invoked to update the geometry of the given end-symbol.

    • Constructor Detail

      • EdgeFigure

        public EdgeFigure(UUID viewUUID,
          float xfrom,
          float yfrom,
          float xto,
          float yto)
        Contruct a new AbstractEdgeFigure.
        Parameters:
        viewUUID - is the UUID of the view that is enclosing this figure.
        xfrom - horizontal position of the begin point for this AbstractEdgeFigure.
        yfrom - vertical position of the begin point for this AbstractEdgeFigure.
        xto - horizontal position of the end point for this AbstractEdgeFigure.
        yto - vertical position of the end point for this AbstractEdgeFigure.

    • Method Detail

      • contains

        public boolean contains(float x,
               float y)
        Description copied from class: AbstractViewComponent
        Reply true if given coords are in the bounds of this Fig. This function takes into account only the bounds of the figure.
        Specified by:
        contains in interface LinearFeature
        Specified by:
        contains in interface ViewComponent
        Overrides:
        contains in class AbstractViewComponent
        Parameters:
        x - horizontal coord.
        y - vertical coord.
        Returns:
        true if the point (x,y) is in this Fig.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(Rectangle2f r)
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponent
        Reply true if the given rectangle is inside this component.
        Specified by:
        contains in interface ViewComponent
        Overrides:
        contains in class AbstractViewComponent
        Parameters:
        r - a rectangle
        Returns:
        true if the given rectangle is inside this component; otherwise false.

      • computeSegmentLengths

        protected void computeSegmentLengths(float[] lengths)
        Compute the lengths of all the segments and put these lengths in the specified array. The size of the specified array must be the same as the number of control points
        Parameters:
        lengths - are the computed lengths.

      • getDistanceToSegmentAt

        public float getDistanceToSegmentAt(int index)
        Replies the distance of the first point of the segment at the specified index from the beginning of the edge.
        Parameters:
        index - is the index of the segment in the edge.
        Returns:
        the distance from the beginning of the edge to the first point of the segment.

      • getSegmentLength

        public float getSegmentLength(int index)
        Replies the size of the segment at the specified index.
        Parameters:
        index - is the index of the segment to consider.
        Returns:
        the size of the segment.

      • getLength

        public float getLength()
        Replies the size of the edge.
        Returns:
        the length of the entire edge.

      • computePath

        protected abstract EdgeFigure.PathDetails computePath(List<? extends Point2D> controlPoints)
        Create and replies the general path that is representing this edge figure. It is recommended to invoke getPath() instead of this method.
        Parameters:
        controlPoints - are the control points that are composing the path.
        Returns:
        the general path.

      • getShadowPainter

        public EdgeShadowPainter getShadowPainter()
        Replies the painter that may be used to paint this figure in shadow mode. You must call ShadowPainter.release() when you have finished to paint the figure in shadow mode.
        Specified by:
        getShadowPainter in interface Figure
        Specified by:
        getShadowPainter in interface LinearFeature
        Overrides:
        getShadowPainter in class AbstractFigure
        Returns:
        the painter, never null

        Parameters:

      • getCtrlPoints

        public List<? extends Point2D> getCtrlPoints()
        Get the edge control points.
        Returns:
        control points of this edge.

      • setFirstCtrlPoint

        public void setFirstCtrlPoint(float x,
                     float y)
        Set the source port point.
        Parameters:
        x - is the coordinate of the first control point.
        y - is the coordinate of the first control point.

      • getFirstCtrlPoint

        public UnmodifiablePoint2f getFirstCtrlPoint()
        Get The source port point.
        Returns:
        the first control point.

      • setLastCtrlPoint

        public void setLastCtrlPoint(float x,
                    float y)
        Set the destination port point.
        Parameters:
        x - is the coordinate of the last control point.
        y - is the coordinate of the last control point.

      • getLastCtrlPoint

        public UnmodifiablePoint2f getLastCtrlPoint()
        Get the destination port point.
        Returns:
        the last control point.

      • getCtrlPointCount

        public int getCtrlPointCount()
        Return the number of point in the path of the edge.
        Specified by:
        getCtrlPointCount in interface LinearFeature
        Returns:
        an integer that is the size of the path.

      • getMaxAllowedCtrlPoints

        public abstract int getMaxAllowedCtrlPoints()
        Return the max number of control point for the edge. Usually two for a linear edge, and Interger.MAX_VALUE for a poly edge.
        Returns:
        an integer that is the max count of control points.

      • getCtrlPointAt

        public Point2f getCtrlPointAt(int index)
        return the point of the path point edge defined by the parameter.
        Specified by:
        getCtrlPointAt in interface LinearFeature
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the point from the path point edge to get. 0 corresponds to the sourcePortPoint and size-1 (where "size" denote the size of the path point list) the destination port point.
        Returns:
        coordinates of the control point.

      • setCtrlPointAt

        public void setCtrlPointAt(int index,
                  float x,
                  float y)
        Set the point of the path defined by the given index.
        Specified by:
        setCtrlPointAt in interface LinearFeature
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the control point in the path.
        x - is new coordinate of the control point.
        y - is new coordinate of the control point.

      • insertCtrlPointAt

        public void insertCtrlPointAt(int index,
                     float x,
                     float y)
        Insert a point into the path defined by the given index.
        Specified by:
        insertCtrlPointAt in interface LinearFeature
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the control point in the path.
        x - is the coordinate of the control point.
        y - is the coordinate of the control point.

      • getNearestSegmentTo

        public int getNearestSegmentTo(float x,
                      float y)
        Replies the index of the nearest segment to the specified point.
        Specified by:
        getNearestSegmentTo in interface LinearFeature
        Returns:
        the index of the nearest segment on the edge. It is always in [-1,LinearFeature.getCtrlPointCount()-2], where the value -1 is when there is no segment in the edge.
        See Also:
        LinearFeature.getNearestCtrlPointTo(float, float)

      • getNearestPointTo

        public Point2D getNearestPointTo(float x,
                        float y)
        Replies the point on the linear feature that is near to the given coordinates.
        Specified by:
        getNearestPointTo in interface LinearFeature
        Parameters:
        x - horizontal location of the point.
        y - vertical location of the point.
        Returns:
        the nearest point on the linear feature, never null.

      • getNearestCtrlPointTo

        public int getNearestCtrlPointTo(float x,
                        float y)
        Replies the index of the nearest control point to the specified point.
        Specified by:
        getNearestCtrlPointTo in interface LinearFeature
        Returns:
        the index of the nearest control point on the edge.
        See Also:
        LinearFeature.getNearestSegmentTo(float, float)

      • removeCtrlPointAt

        public void removeCtrlPointAt(int index)
        Remove a point at the given index from the path.
        Specified by:
        removeCtrlPointAt in interface LinearFeature
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the control point in the path.

      • updateGeometry

        protected void updateGeometry()
        Calculate the bounds of the edge and its associated symbols. This function invokes:
        • cleanUp()
        • computeEdgeBounds()
        • updateSymbolGeometry(EdgeSymbol, int, Point2D, Vector2D) on the both symbols
        • updateAssociatedGeometry()
        • setBounds(float, float, float, float)
          • .

      • computeEdgeBounds

        protected Rectangle2f computeEdgeBounds()
        Invoked to refine the computation of the bounds of the edge's segments. By default this function compute the bounds of the control points themselves. It is defined to be overridden by the subclasses.
        Returns:
        the bounds of the edge's control points; or null if the bounds cannot be computed.

      • updateSymbolGeometry

        protected void updateSymbolGeometry(EdgeSymbol symbol,
                        int indexOfAttachPoint,
                        Point2D attachPoint,
                        Vector2D tangent)
        Invoked to update the geometry of the given end-symbol. By default this function call EdgeSymbol.setGeometry(float, float, float, float). It is defined to be overridden by the subclasses when they need to update the symbol is a specifical way.
        Parameters:
        symbol - is the end-symbol to be updated.
        indexOfAttachPoint - is the index of the point passed in attachPoint.
        attachPoint - is the point where the symbol is attached.
        tangent - is the tangent at the attach point.

      • updateAssociatedGeometry

        protected void updateAssociatedGeometry()
        Invoked to update the geometry of any associated object when the shape of this component has changed. This function may change the coerced figures. This function changes the anchors of the associated coerced figures if, and only if, their description is a number that is corresponding to the position of the anchor on the edge. It is defined to be overridden by the subclasses.
        Specified by:
        updateAssociatedGeometry in class AbstractViewComponent

      • setLocation

        public void setLocation(float x,
               float y)
        Change the position of this Fig.
        Specified by:
        setLocation in interface ViewComponent
        Overrides:
        setLocation in class AbstractViewComponent
        Parameters:
        x - the new horizontal position of this Fig.
        y - the new vertical position of this Fig.

      • translate

        public void translate(float dx,
             float dy)
        Move this Fig.
        Specified by:
        translate in interface ViewComponent
        Overrides:
        translate in class AbstractViewComponent
        Parameters:
        dx - horizontal movement dimension.
        dy - vertical movement dimension.

      • flatteningAt

        public abstract boolean flatteningAt(int index)
        Try to remove the control point at the specified index if its removal does produce a large change in the polyline. This function is useful to rmeove the control points that do not contribute enough to the shape of the edge.
        Specified by:
        flatteningAt in interface LinearFeature
        Returns:
        true if the control point was removed; false if it was not removed.

      • findConnectingPointFromTo

        Point2D findConnectingPointFromTo(Anchor<?,?,?,?> from,
                                Anchor<?,?,?,?> to,
                                boolean isFromStart)
        Compute and replies the position of the end of the edge that is connected to the anchor "from" and the other side of the edge is connected to the anchor "to".
        Parameters:
        from - is the anchor connected to
        to - is the other side of the edge.
        isFromStart - indicates of the from is the start anchor of the edge. This parameter is useful when the "from" and "to" is the same anchor.
        Returns:
        the point of connection to the anchor "from".

      • updateFromModel

        protected void updateFromModel(ModelObjectEvent event)
        Invoked when the associated has model changed.
        Specified by:
        updateFromModel in class AbstractModelObjectFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        event - is the model change event. If null if means that this figure was associated to a new model object.

      • refreshConnectedCtrlPoints

        public void refreshConnectedCtrlPoints()
        Refresh the positions of the connected control points.

      • hitSegment

        public int hitSegment(float x,
             float y,
             float epsilon)
        Replies the index of the nearest segment to the specified point.
        Specified by:
        hitSegment in interface LinearFeature
        epsilon - is the maximal distance from the point to the segment.
        Returns:
        the index of the nearest segment on the edge. It is always in [-1,LinearFeature.getCtrlPointCount()-2], where the value -1 is when there is no segment in the edge.
        See Also:
        LinearFeature.hitCtrlPoint(float, float, float)

      • isClosedToSegment

        protected abstract boolean isClosedToSegment(float x1,
                        float y1,
                        float x2,
                        float y2,
                        float x,
                        float y,
                        float hitDistance)
        Replies if a point is closed to a segment. By default this function calls MathUtil.isPointClosedToSegment(float, float, float, float, float, float, float). This function may be overridden by the subclasses to influence the hitSegment(float, float, float)'s behavior.
        Parameters:
        x1 - horizontal location of the first-segment begining.
        y1 - vertical location of the first-segment ending.
        x2 - horizontal location of the second-segment begining.
        y2 - vertical location of the second-segment ending.
        x - horizontal location of the point.
        y - vertical location of the point.
        hitDistance - is the maximal hitting distance.
        Returns:
        true if the point and the line have closed locations.

      • distanceToSegment

        protected abstract float distanceToSegment(float x1,
                      float y1,
                      float x2,
                      float y2,
                      float x,
                      float y,
                      Point2D pts)
        Replies if a point is closed to a segment. By default this function calls MathUtil.distancePointToSegment(float, float, float, float, float, float). This function may be overridden by the subclasses to influence the getNearestSegmentTo(float, float)'s behavior.
        Parameters:
        x1 - horizontal location of the first-segment begining.
        y1 - vertical location of the first-segment ending.
        x2 - horizontal location of the second-segment begining.
        y2 - vertical location of the second-segment ending.
        x - horizontal location of the point.
        y - vertical location of the point.
        pts - is set with the coordinates of the nearest point, if not null.
        Returns:
        true if the point and the line have closed locations.

      • hitCtrlPoint

        public int hitCtrlPoint(float x,
               float y,
               float epsilon)
        Replies the hit control point at the specified position. The control points at the ends of the edge cannot be hit.
        Specified by:
        hitCtrlPoint in interface LinearFeature
        Parameters:
        x - is the position of the hit point.
        y - is the position of the hit point.
        epsilon - is the error amount allowed for the hitting test.
        Returns:
        the index of the control point that is hit; otherwise -1.
        See Also:
        LinearFeature.hitSegment(float, float, float)

      • paintEdgeSymbols

        protected void paintEdgeSymbols(ViewGraphics2D g)
        Method to paint end-symbols of this Fig.
        Parameters:
        g - the graphic context
        Since:
        0.9

      • paint

        public final void paint(ViewGraphics2D g)
        Method to paint this Fig.
        Specified by:
        paint in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        g - the graphic context.

      • paintSegments

        protected void paintSegments(ViewGraphics2D g,
                 Path2f path)
        Paint the segments represented by the specified control points.
        Parameters:
        g - is the graphical context.
        path - is the path to draw.

      • getStartSymbol

        public EdgeSymbol getStartSymbol()
        Reply the end-symbol connected to the source port.
        Returns:
        the start end-symbol or null if none.

      • setStartSymbol

        public void setStartSymbol(EdgeSymbol symbol)
        Change the end-symbol connected to the source port.
        Parameters:
        symbol - the new end-symbol.

      • getEndSymbol

        public EdgeSymbol getEndSymbol()
        Reply the end-symbol connected to the target port.
        Returns:
        the end end-symbol or null if none.

      • setEndSymbol

        public void setEndSymbol(EdgeSymbol symbol)
        Change the end-symbol connected to the target port.
        Parameters:
        symbol - is the new end-symbol.

        Specified by:
        getProperties in interface PropertyOwner
        Overrides:
        getProperties in class AbstractFigure

        Specified by:
        setProperties in interface PropertyOwner
        Overrides:
        setProperties in class AbstractFigure
